priorityScale.P0: Serious: plausible production compromise, public mutation surface, or integrity break; resolve before beta/production unless explicitly accepted.
priorityScale.P1: Concerning: material exposure or abuse path; reduce before beta when feasible and block production unless acknowledged.
priorityScale.P2: Risky: hardening, minimization, or defense-in-depth gap; track with a concrete owner and release decision.
priorityScale.P3: Warning: hygiene or monitoring issue; keep visible so future code review does not reintroduce it.

productionGatePolicy.summary: Production publish is blocked when a tracked security issue is open and not explicitly acknowledged, or when a tracked issue is marked resolved but is still observed in the production artifact.
productionGatePolicy.betaBehavior: Beta publish shows tracked issues as reminders only.

findings.title: Production bundle exposes harness and debug mutation APIs
findings.summary: Local beta and production artifacts no longer expose browser harness globals, force-write harness symbols, or debug crash globals. Dev still carries the harness for local automation.
findings.nextAction: Keep the public-lane scan active and run a dev harness smoke check after any future harness/build-lane change.
findings.doneDefinition: Production and beta artifact scans no longer find harness globals or force-write symbols, while local/dev harness checks still pass.
proposedPlan: Keep browser harness exports only in dev/test lanes.
Strip public-lane harness export blocks and debug crash globals during production build and beta promotion.
Keep production artifact checks for window.__galagaHarness__, window.__platinumHarness__, and force-write harness flag names.
Keep remote write force flags hard-disabled in production even if a public user defines similarly named globals.

findings.title: Leaderboard write integrity depends on client-trusted score submission
findings.summary: Public beta and production artifacts no longer ship the client-side score insert path. Remote score writes are disabled in public lanes pending server-side validation or a documented RLS policy audit; dev/local harness score-write testing remains available.
findings.nextAction: Before restoring online score writes in beta/production, capture the Supabase RLS/policy state and implement a server-validated score acceptance path.
findings.doneDefinition: Production and beta artifacts do not ship direct client score inserts, or a release artifact records server-side/RLS validation that prevents client-controlled trusted fields.
proposedPlan: Keep public beta/production score writes disabled until score acceptance is server-validated.
Audit Supabase RLS for scores and profiles before re-enabling public writes.
Require authenticated users to write only their own user_id and prevent client-controlled verified fields.
Move score acceptance to a server-side RPC or edge function that validates a signed run summary or replay evidence where practical.

findings.title: Production public artifact exposes reference audio assets
findings.summary: Local beta and production artifacts no longer expose public hrefs to private reference-audio clips or bundle private clip bytes. Hosted public Pages cleanup on 2026-06-10 removed stale root, beta, and dev reference-audio files; known historical clip URLs now return 404 instead of audio bytes.
findings.nextAction: Keep the publish-lane prune guard active and recheck known historical clip URLs after future hosted lane publishes.
findings.doneDefinition: Production and beta artifacts contain no reference-audio clip bytes and no public href/text path that invites fetching private reference clips; dev/local review keeps private provisioning explicit and ignored.
proposedPlan: Keep local/dev reference-audio provisioning through ignored local assets or private artifact bootstrap only.
Keep public-lane runtime and generated docs stripped of private reference-audio hrefs.
Keep artifact-boundary checks active so production fails if dist/production references or contains private reference-audio paths.
Keep the publish-lane prune guard active for root, /beta, and /dev assets/reference-audio directories.

findings.title: Hosted production lacks explicit browser security headers
findings.summary: Local beta and production HTML artifacts now include a generated meta Content-Security-Policy and strict-origin-when-cross-origin referrer policy. GitHub Pages HTTP-header hardening remains a future hosting improvement.
findings.nextAction: After the next hosted beta/dev publish, verify the meta CSP is present in live HTML and capture the remaining host-header limitations.
findings.doneDefinition: The active host emits documented hardening headers or the app ships the strictest feasible meta policy with explicit residual GitHub Pages limitations.
proposedPlan: Keep the generated meta CSP and referrer policy on beta/production HTML artifacts.
Track the residual GitHub Pages limitation that frame-ancestors, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, and Permissions-Policy need real host headers.
When feasible, front the site with a host that can set app-owned security headers.
Reduce unsafe-inline CSP allowances as build packaging moves away from inline bundle/style delivery.

findings.title: Production build metadata exposes test pilot emails and internal release context
findings.summary: Local beta and production artifacts no longer expose non-production test pilot emails or user ids in build-info, release-notes, or inline build metadata. Dev/local review keeps the test-pilot configuration.
findings.nextAction: Keep the public-lane email scan active and verify hosted beta/production after the next publish.
findings.doneDefinition: Beta and production build-info, release-notes, and inline build metadata contain no non-production test pilot emails/user ids, while dev/local auth review still has the needed test-pilot configuration.
proposedPlan: Keep non-production test pilot emails out of beta/production build-info, release-notes, and inline build metadata.
Keep dev/local test-pilot configuration available through the canonical release manifest.
Keep metadata minimization checks in the production publish preflight.
Review remaining commit/branch/build timing metadata separately if release authority wants stricter public minimization.

findings.title: Production bundle mixes dev, beta, and production account/test logic
findings.summary: Local beta and production artifacts now strip public harness/debug exports, strip client score writes, clear test-pilot constants, and hard-disable the account-review lane with NON_PRODUCTION_LANE=false.
findings.nextAction: Keep the hard-disable scan active and add a focused public-lane account smoke before re-enabling any beta test-pilot behavior.
findings.doneDefinition: Production bundle scans no longer find dev/test-only lane symbols unless they are documented as inert public support text; dev/beta behavior remains covered by harnesses.
proposedPlan: Keep lane-specific dev/test controls stripped or hard-disabled in beta/production at build/promotion time.
Keep production account and leaderboard logic minimal and explicit.
Keep the production bundle scan for active dev/test-only symbols.
Document any intentionally retained diagnostics as read-only support surfaces.
